title: Add Areas of Decomposed Parts
description: When a figure is decomposed into non-overlapping shapes, add their individual areas to find the total area.
condition: A composite figure is partitioned into non-overlapping rectangles and/or triangles with known areas.
response: Add the areas of all component shapes to obtain the total area.
label: Principle
example: If a figure is split into a rectangle of area $24$ and a triangle of area $10$, total area $=24+10=34$.

title: Area of a Parallelogram
description: Compute the area of a parallelogram as base times corresponding height.
condition: A parallelogram with a known base and its perpendicular height.
response: Calculate $A=b\times h$.
label: Principle
example: If $b=6$ cm and $h=4$ cm, then $A=6\times4=24\text{ cm}^2$.

title: Area of a Rectangle
description: Compute the area of a rectangle by multiplying length by width.
condition: A rectangle with given length and width.
response: Calculate $A=l\times w$.
label: Principle
example: If $l=26$ and $w=10$, then $A=26\times10=260$.

title: Area of a Square
description: Compute the area of a square by squaring the side length.
condition: A square with given side length.
response: Calculate A = s^2.
label: Principle / Model
example: If side length is 4, then $A = 4^2 = 16$.
notes: Used when small triangles are rearranged into a square.

title: Area of a Triangle
description: Compute the area of a triangle as one-half the product of base and corresponding height.
condition: A triangle with known base and perpendicular height.
response: Calculate $A=\tfrac{1}{2}bh$.
label: Principle
example: If $b=20$ and $h=10$, then $A=\tfrac{1}{2}\cdot20\cdot10=100$.
notes: [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Compare Computed Area to Given Limit
description: Determine if a computed total area is less than or equal to a given coverage amount.
condition: A calculated total area and a given maximum coverage value.
response: Compare values and state whether the total area is less than or equal to the limit.
label: Principle
example: If total area is $720$ and coverage is $800$, since $720<800$, it is enough.

title: Count Unit Squares on Grid
description: Find area by counting full unit squares (and combining partial squares) on a grid.
condition: A shape drawn on a square grid with unit squares.
response: Count the number of square units covering the region.
label: Production / Skill
example: If a shaded region covers 12 full grid squares, the area is $12$ square units.

title: Decompose Composite Figure
description: Break a complex polygon into simpler shapes (rectangles/triangles) to facilitate area calculation.
condition: A composite polygon whose area is not directly given by a single formula.
response: Partition the figure into rectangles and/or triangles with computable dimensions.
label: Production / Skill
example: Enclose an irregular shape in a $6\times9$ rectangle and subtract triangular parts.

title: Decompose Polygon into Triangles
description: Partition a composite polygon into triangles (or other familiar shapes) to simplify area calculation.
condition: A polygon’s area is difficult to compute directly.
response: Draw segments to divide the polygon into triangles or rectangles with known area formulas.
label: Principle / Model
example: Split a polygon into two right triangles; compute each area separately.
notes: Decomposition must preserve total area without overlap.

title: Different Base–Height Pairs Yield Same Area
description: Understand that any valid base–height pair for a parallelogram produces the same area.
condition: Given a parallelogram with more than one possible base and corresponding height pair.
response: Recognize that calculating b \times h for either pair gives the same area.
label: Concept
example: Using b = 4 cm and h = 8 cm gives 32 cm^2, and using b = 10 cm and h = 3.2 cm also gives 10 \times 3.2 = 32 cm^2.
notes: Follows from the geometric structure of parallelograms.

title: Draw Height Perpendicular to Base
description: Given a chosen base of a triangle, draw the corresponding height as a perpendicular segment from the opposite vertex (possibly extending the base).
condition: A triangle with a specified base.
response: Draw a segment from the opposite vertex perpendicular to the line containing the base and label it as height.
label: Production / Skill
example: From the top vertex, draw a perpendicular to the base line to form a right angle.
notes: Height may fall outside the triangle.

title: Ensure Parallelograms Are Not Identical
description: When drawing multiple parallelograms with equal area, ensure they are not congruent copies.
condition: Instruction to draw two parallelograms with equal area that are not identical.
response: Produce two shapes with equal area but different side lengths or angles.
label: Rule / Plan
example: Draw a $4\times4$ rectangle (area $16$) and a parallelogram with $b=8$, $h=2$ (area $16$).

title: Evaluate Small Whole-Number Exponents
description: Evaluate expressions where a whole number is raised to a small exponent such as 2 or 3.
condition: Expression of the form a^n with small whole-number exponent (e.g., 2 or 3)
response: Multiply a by itself n times
label: Principle / Model

title: Exponent 2 Represents Squaring
description: Recognize that raising a number to the second power represents multiplying the number by itself.
condition: Expression of the form a^2
response: Interpret as a \u00b7 a
label: Rule / Plan
notes: Based on definition of exponent notation.

title: Identify Corresponding Base and Height
description: Recognize which measurements form a perpendicular base–height pair for area calculation.
condition: Multiple side lengths are given in a parallelogram or triangle.
response: Select the base and the perpendicular height (not a slanted side) for use in area formula.
label: Principle
example: In a parallelogram with side $5$ and height $4$, use $b=6$ and $h=4$, not the slanted side $5$.
notes: [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Identify Lateral Rectangles of a Triangular Prism
description: Recognize that each side of the triangular base forms a rectangular lateral face whose dimensions are the side length and the prism length.
condition: Given a triangular prism with triangular base side lengths a, b, c and prism length L.
response: Form three rectangles with dimensions a×L, b×L, and c×L.
label: Principle / Model
example: With triangle sides 5, 12, 13 and prism length 8, the rectangles are 5×8, 12×8, and 13×8.

title: Interpret Cube Volume from Exponential Form
description: Recognize that a cube's volume is given by s^3 and interpret the edge length from that expression.
condition: Given volume of a cube expressed as s^3
response: Identify edge length as s
label: Principle / Model
example: If a cube's volume is $2^3$, then its edge length is $2$.

title: Match Net Faces to Prism Faces
description: Determine whether a net can form a given prism by verifying it contains the correct number and shapes of faces.
condition: Given a proposed net and a specified prism with known face shapes and dimensions.
response: Check that the net has two congruent triangular bases and three rectangular lateral faces with matching side lengths.
label: Principle / Model
example: A triangular prism with triangular sides 5, 12, 13 and length 8 must have 2 triangles (5-12-13) and 3 rectangles with dimensions 8×5, 8×12, and 8×13.

title: Match Net Faces to Pyramid Faces
description: Determine whether a net represents a given pyramid by verifying the base and triangular faces match the given dimensions.
condition: Given a square pyramid with base side length s and slant height h.
response: Construct or verify a net with one square of side s and four congruent triangles each with base s and height h.
label: Principle / Model
example: For base 4 and slant height 7, draw a square of side 4 and attach four triangles each with base 4 and height 7.

title: Multiply Base and Height
description: Multiply numerical base and height values as part of area computation.
condition: Numeric values for base and height are given.
response: Compute the product of the two numbers.
label: Production / Skill
example: Compute $6\times4=24$.

title: Multiply Numerical Expressions
description: Multiply given numerical factors accurately to evaluate an area expression.
condition: A numerical expression involving multiplication (and possibly $\tfrac{1}{2}$) is given.
response: Carry out the multiplication to find the product.
label: Principle / Model
example: Compute $\tfrac{1}{2}\cdot14\cdot12 = \tfrac{1}{2}\cdot168 = 84$.

title: Rearrange Pieces to Form Known Shape
description: Reposition congruent pieces (e.g., triangles) to create a rectangle or square to simplify area calculation.
condition: Congruent pieces from a composite figure that can be recombined.
response: Rearrange pieces to form a rectangle or square and compute its area.
label: Production / Skill
example: Combine two congruent right triangles to form a $3 \times 3$ square with area $9$.
notes: Relies on spatial reasoning about congruence.

title: Recognize Height as Perpendicular Segment
description: Understand that the height of a triangle is the segment perpendicular to the chosen base from the opposite vertex.
condition: A base of a triangle has been chosen.
response: Identify or construct the segment from the opposite vertex that forms a right angle with the base.
label: Concept
example: Given base $b$, the height $h$ satisfies $h \perp b$.
notes: The height may lie inside or outside the triangle.

title: Represent Surface Area as Repeated Addition
description: Express the total surface area of a cube as the sum of the areas of its six congruent square faces.
condition: Given a cube with edge length s and asked for surface area expression
response: Write s^2 + s^2 + s^2 + s^2 + s^2 + s^2 (or equivalent repeated products)
label: Principle / Model

title: Solve for Missing Height
description: Find the unknown height of a parallelogram when the area and base are known.
condition: Given the area A and base b of a parallelogram, with unknown height h.
response: Compute h = A \div b.
label: Principle / Model
example: If A = 60 \text{ in}^2 and b = 5 \text{ in}, then h = 60 \div 5 = 12 \text{ in}.
notes: Derived algebraically from A = b \times h.

title: Subtract Areas Not Included
description: When enclosing a figure in a larger shape, subtract the areas of regions that are not part of the target figure.
condition: A composite figure is enclosed in a larger rectangle with identifiable extra regions.
response: Compute the area of the large shape and subtract the areas of the extra parts.
label: Principle
example: If enclosing rectangle has area $54$ and outside triangles total $16$, target area $=54-16=38$.

title: Sum Areas for Surface Area
description: Find total surface area by adding the areas of all faces of a polyhedron.
condition: Given the areas of each face of a prism or pyramid.
response: Add all face areas to obtain total surface area.
label: Principle / Model

title: Surface Area of a Cube
description: The surface area of a cube equals 6 times the area of one square face.
condition: Given a cube with edge length s
response: Compute 6 \u00b7 s^2
label: Principle / Model
example: If a cube has edge length 47, its surface area is $6 \cdot 47^2$.
